reason: SSE replay via /sync/* not implemented in Phase A
/experimental/* endpoints best-effort; not gated until Phase D

Provider OAuth is implemented for built-in providers (plan 13, #109): PKCE auth-code flow over a shared 127.0.0.1 loopback callback with CSRF state, token exchange → shared auth.json (xAI is the first provider; --oauth-callback-proxy-url for headless). Not yet byte-parity-gated by a dual-run scenario (the flow opens a browser + needs live provider creds). Deferred: token refresh and additional providers.
track: done for built-in providers (plan 13); refresh + more providers later

reason: GET /provider is implemented (models.dev catalog + connected/default). It is NOT byte-parity with opencode: `all` matches, but `connected` depends on which provider credentials are in the daemon's env/auth.json (opencode also detects via per-provider SDK init). Membership/ids are comparable; not gated by a dual-run scenario.
track: done (plan 04 M7/M8); refine oauth detection later

reason: GET /agent is implemented (built-ins + .opencode/agent(s) + config). It is NOT byte-parity with opencode: built-in agents carry a simple allow-all permission ruleset rather than opencode's env-specific patterns (tool-output/temp dirs) and full system prompts, Forge omits flag-gated built-ins (scout), and the config `agent` overlay applies only description/mode/hidden/model (not prompt/temperature/options/permission). The self-diff gate is opencode-vs-opencode so this does not affect CI.
track: done (plan 04 M3/M8); richer built-in permissions later

reason: GET /command is implemented (.opencode/command(s) + config). Forge returns only file/config commands (source=command); opencode also surfaces built-in, MCP, and skill-derived commands. The opencode order is also non-deterministic so it was never a parity scenario.
track: done for .opencode commands (plan 04 M4/M8); skill/MCP commands are plan 04 M6 / plan 03

reason: GET /find/file approximates opencode two ways, so it is not gated by a parity scenario: (1) ignore semantics — opencode delegates to `rg --files` (full .gitignore); Forge uses a fixed ignore set (.git/node_modules/vendor/.forge + hidden), so results may include files .gitignore would exclude. (2) ranking — opencode uses fuzzysort; Forge uses its own subsequence scorer, so result ORDER differs (membership is comparable, order is not). The missing-query 400 body is Forge's {_tag,message} rather than opencode's Effect Query error.
track: TODO: optional — vendor a .gitignore-aware walker for exact membership parity (plan 04)

reason: The `task` subagent tool is wired (child session linked to parent, nested agent loop, last-text result wrapped in opencode's <task>/<task_result> envelope). One parity gap: opencode also disables `todowrite` on the child unless the agent's permission grants it (tool/task.ts:194); Forge only disables `task` (nil subagent → bounded recursion). Honoring per-message tool disables needs PromptInput.Tools wired into the registry filter (not yet consumed), so it is deferred. Child session titles also use the generic timestamp rather than opencode's '<desc> (@agent subagent)'.
track: TODO: wire per-message Tools gating + descriptive child titles (plan 02 follow-up)

reason: System instructions from AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/CONTEXT.md (project upward-walk + global ~/.config/opencode + ~/.claude) and local config `instructions[]` are resolved into the system prompt. http(s) URLs in `instructions[]` are NOT fetched (opencode fetches them; instruction.ts:154-159) — remote instruction fetch is deferred. The `disable_claude_code_prompt` flag is also not honored yet (CLAUDE.md is always a candidate).
track: TODO: fetch remote instruction URLs + honor disable_claude_code_prompt (plan 04)

reason: GET /skill loads skills from: .opencode {skill,skills}/**/SKILL.md (project + ~/.config/opencode), the external ~/.claude and ~/.agents skills/**/SKILL.md dirs (global + project found-up), and config skills.paths[] (**/SKILL.md). Name from frontmatter (no-name skipped), content = body, last-wins dedup. Remaining gaps vs opencode: (1) no built-in/embedded skills (opencode bundles e.g. customize-opencode, location '<built-in>'); (2) no remote skills.urls[] discovery/pull + cache; (3) the disableExternalSkills/disableClaudeCodeSkills flags aren't honored. Not gated by a parity scenario.
track: TODO: embed built-in skills + remote skills.urls[] pull (plan 04 M6)

reason: Credential CRUD is implemented: PUT/DELETE /auth/{providerID} read+write opencode's shared ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json (api/oauth/wellknown records), so a key added in Forge flips /provider.connected and is shared with opencode. The OAuth authorize/callback flow is now implemented for built-in providers (plan 13, #109: PKCE auth-code over a shared loopback callback, token exchange → auth.json). Deferred: GET /provider/auth (the per-provider auth-method listing), token refresh, and additional OAuth providers.
track: OAuth done for built-in providers (plan 13); GET /provider/auth method listing + refresh later (plan 04 M7/M8)

reason: The websearch tool is wired against Exa/Parallel hosted MCP endpoints (env-gated: OPENCODE_WEBSEARCH_PROVIDER / EXA_API_KEY / PARALLEL_API_KEY); without a key it returns a clear 'not configured' error. Forge passes only the query (defaults: type=auto, numResults=8, livecrawl=fallback); it does not forward opencode's numResults/livecrawl/type/contextMaxCharacters params, the session_id/model_name to Parallel, or apply opencode's session-checksum default-provider selection (Forge defaults to whichever key is present). It is not behind a permission ask.
track: TODO: forward full search params + session/model context (plan 04)

reason: MCP (plan 03): config (mcp.* local/remote) is parsed per-instance; BOTH local (stdio) and remote servers connect lazily — remote uses StreamableHTTP with SSE fallback (opencode's transport order, mcp/index.ts:339-413); GET /mcp reports {connected|disabled|failed} status; tools are merged into the agent loop — flattened as sanitize(server)_sanitize(tool), exposed to the model, and dispatched via the executor (registry-miss → MCP); a notifications/tools/list_changed watcher re-lists tools and emits the mcp.tools.changed bus event; and MCP tool execution is permission-gated through the same ask/Ask path as built-ins (keyed on the flattened tool name, patterns/always '*', emitting permission.asked — asserted by conformance/mcp_permission_test.go). Not yet implemented: (1) OAuth + the needs_auth/needs_client_registration statuses (an auth failure currently surfaces as 'failed'); (2) mutating endpoints (POST /mcp add, /mcp/:name/connect|disconnect|auth) remain 501.
track: TODO: OAuth (needs_auth/needs_client_registration statuses) + mutating endpoints (plan 03 M3-2)
